opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on intraday pullbacks into 158-165 that hold above 152 with volume confirming buyers on the retest of the gap zone.
opportunities.rationale: Oracle gapped ~9-10% higher on March 10 after beating Q3 FY26 estimates with strong cloud and backlog metrics. The gap-follow-through setup is alive: pullbacks into the 158-165 range offer a controlled entry toward the mid-170s-180s. The cloud re-rating thesis remains intact; stop at 152 (gap low). This is the only post-earnings gap play in the current cluster.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Results drop Monday pre-market. Enter ONLY post-print if Q4 shows stabilising traffic trends or positive Family Dollar separation update. Do NOT pre-position; binary risk is very high given recent -22% crash.
opportunities.rationale: Dollar Tree reports Q4 FY2025 Monday March 16 (pre-market). Stock is down ~22% from its high after missing Q4 last quarter and guiding to a multi-year store restructuring. In a stagflation environment discount retail outperforms as consumers trade down — this is the bull case. Consensus EPS ~$1.93. A beat that confirms traffic stabilisation is the entry trigger; miss widens the post-crash base and invalidates the trade.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Results drop Tuesday after close. Enter ONLY post-print on a confirmed U.S. comparable sales beat or a positive CEO search update. Exit if comps miss or FY2027 guidance disappoints.
opportunities.rationale: LULU is down ~19% YTD to 52-week lows; CEO vacancy, product transition year, and Elliott Management pressure are the known bears. Q4 FY2026 consensus EPS $4.74 (down 22.8% YoY); LULU has beaten in 4 of last 4 quarters including a +16.7% beat in Q3. Median analyst PT $200 vs spot $158 implies 27% upside. The asymmetry of a beat from this oversold level is attractive; the downside on a miss is limited given how low expectations already are. Size at 2% max.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Results drop Tuesday after close. Enter ONLY post-print if Q4 beats consensus EPS $0.95 and revenue guidance is maintained or raised. All 10 covering analysts are Strong Buy with avg PT $197.80.
opportunities.rationale: DocuSign is down ~28% from its recent high after a guidance reset. Q4 FY2026 earnings March 17 after close. Jefferies PT $225, JPMorgan $230 — both maintained despite the reset. The agreement lifecycle management (ALM) platform is gaining traction beyond e-signatures. Stabilisation above 59 is the key technical prerequisite; a Q4 beat from this oversold level could trigger a gap-up similar to the ORCL setup.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Build a position on dips into 104-112 between now and 17 Mar close. Post-print, hold IF management re-affirms HBM demand and AI data centre ramp guidance; cut immediately on a downside revenue guide.
opportunities.rationale: Micron reports Q2 FY2026 on March 18 (after close). Analysts expect revenue to more than double YoY to ~$19B, driven by HBM memory for AI training and NAND recovery. Susquehanna raised PT to $525 citing peak earnings by mid-2027. This is the AI infrastructure bellwether of the week; a strong print validates the broader AI trade. MU has beaten EPS for 4 consecutive quarters. HIGH conviction on the pre-earnings setup; reduce to 50% before the print to manage binary risk.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on pullbacks into 144-153 that hold above 133 on 15-17 Mar. Exit all positions before the 18 Mar pre-market report OR hold through the print if the macro environment stabilises.
opportunities.rationale: Williams-Sonoma fell ~18% on cautious FY2026 guidance and tariff concerns. Reports Q4 FY2026 pre-market March 18. WSM has beaten EPS 7 of last 8 quarters. RSI below 32. The pre-earnings run-up pattern is well-established for WSM; mean reversion toward $175-180 on a beat is the base case. Stop at 133 (below crash low of 135). Moderate conviction given the macro overlay from FOMC on the same day.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter ONLY post-print if Q3 FY2026 shows organic sales stabilising or guidance is maintained. Do not pre-position given the recent -14% crash.
opportunities.rationale: General Mills reports Q3 FY2026 on March 18 (pre-market). Stock dropped ~14% on March 5 on flat organic sales guidance. In a stagflation environment defensive staples historically recover; GIS 4.1% dividend yield provides a floor. RSI hit 27 at the low. A Q3 beat that shows any green shoots on pricing power or volume recovery triggers mean reversion toward the pre-crash 68 level.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on pullbacks into 240-252 between 16-18 Mar if price holds above 228; exit fully before the 19 Mar post-market report.
opportunities.rationale: FedEx reports Q3 FY2026 on March 19 (after close). The stock has been under pressure from logistics volume softness and fuel cost headwinds. However, FedEx's ongoing DRIVE cost-reduction programme targets $4B in savings by FY2027. A beat on operating margin is the catalyst for a run-up toward $278. Energy cost spike is a double-edged sword: it may pressure COGS but higher fuel surcharges also offset the impact. Exit before the print to avoid binary risk.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on consolidation or shallow dips into 130-137 between 15-18 Mar if price holds above 125; exit all positions before the 19 Mar pre-market earnings release.
opportunities.rationale: Alibaba reports its December-quarter results before the US open on March 19. Stock has sold off from $150-160 to the mid-130s despite upbeat AI cloud growth and regulatory easing in China. Taobao/Tmall GMV recovery and Alibaba Cloud AI growth are the key metrics to watch. The setup is a short pre-earnings re-rating trade from oversold levels; the risk is China macro deceleration and ongoing geopolitical headwinds with the Iran conflict affecting EM sentiment.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Build a position on dips into 195-206 between 15-18 Mar if price holds above 185; exit fully before the 19 Mar report.
opportunities.rationale: Accenture reports Q2 FY2026 on March 19. The stock is near multi-year lows after a sustained de-rating despite strong Advanced AI bookings of $2.2B last quarter. ACN is the bellwether for enterprise IT spend and AI consulting demand. Consensus expects in-line results; the setup is a short pre-earnings mean-reversion bounce with expectations already reset. An upside surprise on AI bookings growth could re-rate the stock materially. Moderate conviction given macro cycle uncertainty.
